articles.title: Roblox’s daily users continue to drop as age-checks slow growth
articles.description:
Roblox's daily active users continued to slip last quarter due in part to its rollout of age checks on its platform. According to its latest earnings report, Roblox currently has 132 million daily active users globally, down from 144 million at the end of last year, which was a drop from 152 million in Q3 2025. In the US and Canada, the number of active users dropped by one million from the previous quarter, while Roblox's revenue still grew to $1.4 billion.
Roblox says Q1 growth was "tempered by greater-than-expected headwinds" due to the rollout of its age-check features, which "slowed new user acquisition." Russia's December 2025 ban on  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Congress keeps kicking surveillance reform down the road
articles.description:
Congress has reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act - but only for another 45 days. The extension is meant to give legislators more time to negotiate reforms to the controversial wiretapping bill. If the past few weeks are any indication of how future debates will go, however, we're in for a bumpy ride.
The House renewed Section 702 with minor reforms on Wednesday evening. The bill didn't include the hotly debated warrant requirement, but it did feature a provision prohibiting the Federal Reserve from issuing Central Bank Digital Currencies, which Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD) described as a nonstar …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Apple’s iPhone revenue jumps to $57 billion despite chip shortages
articles.description:
Apple's iPhone revenue jumped 22 percent to $57 billion over the past few months, despite supply chain issues impacting the device's processor. During an interview with Reuters, Apple CEO Tim Cook said iPhone "demand was off the charts" but added that there's "a little less flexibility in the supply chain at the moment for getting more parts."
Apple earned $111.2 billion in revenue during the second quarter of 2026, its "best March quarter ever," according to Cook. This latest earnings report comes a little over a week after the company announced CEO Cook's retirement in September. John Ternus, the company's head of hardware engineering, wi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: GOAT in Kashmir: Swiss Saanen sparks milk rush, but wait is long
articles.description: Srinagar's agricultural university has welcomed Swiss Saanen goats, sparking immense interest for their high milk yield with less feed. These docile animals, capable of producing five to six litres daily, are being studied for their adaptability to Kashmir's climate and potential for crossbreeding with local breeds. Researchers believe they could revolutionize dairy farming, even for urban households.

articles.title: Rivian’s revenue is up as R2 production kicks into gear
articles.description:
Rivian reported its first quarter earnings of 2026, providing us a closer look at the company's financial health as it kicks off production for the crucial R2 electric vehicle. 
We've already got Rivian's production and delivery statement from the first three months of the year. The company sold 10,365 vehicles in Q1, representing 20 percent increase year over year. And it produced 10,236 vehicles at its factory in Normal, Illinois, which represents a 30 percent increase compared to the same period last year. The company also reaffirmed its prediction that it will sell 62,000-67,000 vehicles this year.
In terms of earnings, Rivian said it  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Rivian downsizes its goals for its EV factory in Georgia
articles.description:
Rivian announced some changes today with regard to the factory its building in the state of Georgia. The company was planning to build the facility in two phases, each resulting in 200,000 vehicles of annual production capacity, for a total of 400,000 units. 
Now the company says it is only planning for 300,000 units of annual capacity as a result of a revised loan agreement with the US Department of Energy - though its planning on hitting that annual capacity sooner than originally planned. DOE will now loan Rivian $4.5 billion, instead of the originally agreed amount of $6.6 billion, announced in the last days of the Biden administration. …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: The logic of the racist Supreme Court isn’t adding up
articles.description:
Close watchers of the Supreme Court knew that the conservative supermajority was about to murder what was left of the Voting Rights Act. Wednesday's decision in Louisiana v. Callais took down Section 2 of the law, clearing the way for racist gerrymandering, because it is now racist to remedy racism. The decision is an affront to the history of the Voting Rights Act, an affront to the history of the United States, and an affront to math. 
The state of Louisiana, which is around 30 percent Black, has six districts. The voting districts are drawn so that there are two majority-Black districts. That is two out of six districts; approximately 33 …

articles.title: Trump officials flew on 1st non-stop commercial flight from US to Venezuela in years
articles.description: The trip comes nearly four months after U.S. forces seized Rodríguez's predecessor, Nicolás Maduro​, and his wife in a daring special forces raid.

articles.title: Ex-federal official worried U.S. "may not come out on top" if Iran war becomes "battle of endurance"
articles.description: The ceasefire with Iran is holding, but negotiations are stalled. The ripple effects: tight energy supplies, price spikes and recession risks. Edward Fishman, former Russian and Europe sanctions lead at the State Department, joins with analysis.

articles.title: "Ozempic breath" is boosting Hershey's sales of mints and gum
articles.description: Hershey says it's benefiting from the growing use of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs even as people cut down on snacks. Here's why.

articles.title: After Supreme Court Decision, Louisiana Weighs Redrawing House Maps
articles.description: The Louisiana Secretary of State said the House primary election would be delayed after the court ruled the state’s congressional districts unconstitutional. The Senate primary will go forward on May 16.

articles.title: Remembering J. Craig Venter: a relentless scientist who changed biotech — and was all too easily misunderstood
articles.description: J. Craig Venter, the scientist whose relentless ambition helped turn genetics from an artisanal trade into an industrialized information machine, died at 79.

articles.title: Top court directs Centre to regularise daily wage workers at Isro in 4 weeks
articles.description: The Supreme Court ordered the Centre to regularise ISRO's daily wage workers within four weeks, highlighting their vital contributions to India's space missions.

articles.title: Phillies players viciously booed by Philadelphia faithful at Flyers playoff game
articles.description: The Philadelphia Phillies were booed after being shown on the jumbotron during the Flyers' playoff win over the Pittsburgh Penguins amid their brutal start.

articles.title: Kanha reserve: Viral outbreak suspected in deaths of tigress, cubs
articles.description: A tigress and her four cubs died in Kanha Tiger Reserve, raising fears of a Canine Distemper Virus outbreak; monitoring and preventive measures are underway.

topStories.title: Black GOP congressman shuts down race question following Supreme Court redistricting ruling
topStories.description: Rep. Wesley Hunt pushed back on questions about Black Republicans in Congress after the Supreme Court's Voting Rights Act ruling about a Louisiana congressional district.

topStories.title: Kris Jenner addresses rumors she’s unhappy with reported $100K facelift
topStories.description: Kris Jenner calls rumors she is unhappy with her facelift a flat out lie, saying she adores Dr. Steven LeVine and his work on her reported $100,000 procedure.

topStories.title: Palestine FA chief hits out at Israel federation VP at FIFA Congress
topStories.description: Palestine and Israel representatives had been lined up close together at the FIFA Congress in Canada.

topStories.title: D4vd killed Celeste Rivas Hernandez to "silence" her, prosecutors allege
topStories.description: The singer is accused of killing 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

topStories.title: Watch: Sen. Gary Peters presses Hegseth on the need to reopen of Strait of Hormuz
topStories.description: Democratic Sen. Gary Peters told Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth the U.S. needs to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. "We're not going to bring this war to an end until we seize control of the Strait in a way that opens it back up," Peters said. Hegseth responded by pointing to the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports.

topStories.title: Millie Bobby Brown fans call out ‘Enola Holmes 3’ for glaring manicure mistake
topStories.description: Fans call out Millie Bobby Brown's manicure in the first "Enola Holmes 3" trailer, questioning almond-shaped nails in a 19th-century setting.

dailyNews.title: Students stabbed inside Washington state high school as police respond to chaotic scene
dailyNews.description: At least five people were stabbed at Foss High School in Tacoma, Washington, sending victims to the hospital as police rushed to secure the scene and investigate the attack.

dailyNews.title: Britney Spears charged with DUI after driving under the influence of alcohol, drugs
dailyNews.description: Britney Spears was charged with driving under the influence of drugs and alcohol in California. The pop star, who has entered treatment, was arrested after erratic driving. Her arraignment is set for Monday, 4 May.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court asks petitioner to turn in first-generation copy of leaked Manipur audio to forensic lab
dailyNews.description: The forensic reports have so far returned inconclusive about the authenticity of the tapes

dailyNews.description: American Airlines has resumed flights as Donald Trump moves to rebuild ties following the abduction of Nicolas Maduro.

dailyNews.description: Price of petrol in US jumps by nearly 30 cents in one week amid Strait of Hormuz blockade and Iran diplomatic deadlock.

dailyNews.title: Roblox’s daily users continue to drop as age-checks slow growth
dailyNews.description:
Roblox's daily active users continued to slip last quarter due in part to its rollout of age checks on its platform. According to its latest earnings report, Roblox currently has 132 million daily active users globally, down from 144 million at the end of last year, which was a drop from 152 million in Q3 2025. In the US and Canada, the number of active users dropped by one million from the previous quarter, while Roblox's revenue still grew to $1.4 billion.
Roblox says Q1 growth was "tempered by greater-than-expected headwinds" due to the rollout of its age-check features, which "slowed new user acquisition." Russia's December 2025 ban on  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: Who is Nicole Saphier? Trump’s new Surgeon General nominee after Casey Means’ exit
dailyNews.description: Trump announced the decision on April 30, praising Saphier as “a STAR physician” with extensive experience in breast cancer diagnosis and treatment.

dailyNews.title: FISA Section 702: Congress passes short-term surveillance program extension just before deadline
dailyNews.description: The controversial foreign surveillance program was due to expire by the end of Thursday.

dailyNews.title: Britney Spears charged with DUI after arrest last month in Southern California
dailyNews.description: Pop star Britney Spears has been charged in Southern California with driving under the influence, the Ventura County District Attorney’s Office said Thursday.

dailyNews.title: Congress keeps kicking surveillance reform down the road
dailyNews.description:
Congress has reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act - but only for another 45 days. The extension is meant to give legislators more time to negotiate reforms to the controversial wiretapping bill. If the past few weeks are any indication of how future debates will go, however, we're in for a bumpy ride.
The House renewed Section 702 with minor reforms on Wednesday evening. The bill didn't include the hotly debated warrant requirement, but it did feature a provision prohibiting the Federal Reserve from issuing Central Bank Digital Currencies, which Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD) described as a nonstar …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.description: Examining the past statements and writings of Trump's third surgeon general nominee, Dr. Nicole Saphier, on vaccines, autism, peptides, diet and more.

topStories.title: After Germany, Trump Says US Considering Reducing Troops In Italy, Spain
topStories.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday he may pull US troops from Italy and Spain due to their opposition to the Iran war, a day after proposing a similar reduction in Germany.

topStories.title: Trump pulls Casey Means' stalled surgeon general nomination, announces new pick
topStories.description: President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.

topStories.title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
topStories.description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

topStories.title: Hegseth argues Iran ceasefire ‘pauses’ deadline for Congress’s approval
topStories.description: The War Powers Act requires that the Trump administration seek lawmakers’ consent to continue hostilities beyond 60 days, a time limit that expires Friday.

topStories.title: Mapping where the redistricting fight stands and where it’s headed
topStories.description: As Trump pushes for a more Republican-friendly House map, more than half a dozen states are potential targets for mid-decade tweaks to congressional boundaries.

topStories.title: King Charles set to return home with a quiet sense of mission accomplished
topStories.description: LONDON — As King Charles III closed out his four-day state visit to the U.S. on Thursday, President Donald Trump issued a three-word assessment of the monarch.“The greatest king,” Trump said after he and first lady Melania Trump said goodbye to Charles and Queen Camilla, an appraisal that explains why Charles could well be returning to the U.K. feeling a quiet sense of mission accomplished

dailyNews.title: Kimmel says Donald and Melania Trump are ‘closer than ever’ after ‘expectant widow’ joke backlash
dailyNews.description: Jimmy Kimmel continued his mockery of President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump on air, joking about their relationship after his controversial "expectant widow" comment.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighs in on LIV Golf, PGA Tour after Saudis pull funding of startup league
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump reacted to LIV Golf losing Saudi PIF funding, saying golfers should reunite on the PGA Tour, and fans want to see the best players face each other.

dailyNews.title: Trump ends DHS' months-long nightmare that left immigration enforcement without funding
dailyNews.description: President Trump signed a bill ending a record-setting 75-day DHS funding lapse, though ICE and Customs and Border Protection remain unfunded.

dailyNews.title: Trump blasts New York Times, CNN for 'seditious' coverage of Iran war
dailyNews.description: During remarks in the Oval Office, President Donald Tump blasted The New York Times and CNN for their coverage of the Iran war, suggesting it's "seditious."

dailyNews.title: FCC boss Brendan Carr maintains early ABC license renewal is about DEI probe, not Jimmy Kimmel controversy
dailyNews.description: FCC Chairman Carr says the early renewal of Disney's ABC licenses is tied to a DEI review, not President Trump's call for Jimmy Kimmel's firing over his controversial joke.

dailyNews.title: Trump Picks Nicole Saphier for Surgeon General After Withdrawing Casey Means Nomination
dailyNews.description: Dr. Means’s nomination had stalled in part over her views on vaccines. The president said he was instead nominating Dr. Nicole B. Saphier, a radiologist at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center.

dailyNews.title: How Trump’s Iran Blockade Is Complicating a High-Stakes Trip to China
dailyNews.description: If President Trump flies to China as planned in May, the primary topic will clearly be the rippling economic effects of a war that Beijing has made clear it viewed as unnecessary.

dailyNews.title: Decoding the King: Brits Hear Subtle Rebuke to Trump that Americans Might Miss
dailyNews.description: Royal watchers in Britain called the visit of King Charles III to America a master class in understated criticism.

dailyNews.title: Sheinbaum’s Dilemma in Mexico: Defy the U.S. or Arrest an Ally
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration wants President Claudia Sheinbaum to arrest a Mexican governor. She is faced with few good options in response.

dailyNews.title: After Seven Years, First U.S. Direct Flight Lands in Caracas
dailyNews.description: President Trump banned commercial flights to Venezuela during his first term, but the flights are the latest step in re-establishing ties between the two countries.

dailyNews.title: Trump signs bill funding DHS, ending record-breaking 76-day shutdown
dailyNews.description: The longest shutdown of a federal department in U.S. history came to an end on Thursday when President Trump signed a bill to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security following a breakthrough on Capitol Hill.

dailyNews.title: Trump drops Casey Means as surgeon general nominee, names Nicole Saphier
dailyNews.description: President Trump picked Dr. Nicole Saphier as his new nominee for surgeon general, and blamed Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy for Casey Mean's nomination stalling in the Senate.
